title: Non-unitary extension of Grover's search algorithm
authors: V. N. A. Lula-Rocha, M. A. S. Trindade
abstract: We have developed a non-unitary extension of Grover's search algorithm by changing the hidden geometry of Hilbert space carried by diffusion operator. Our algorithm finds the solution for search problem by performing a unique bigger rotation rather than small rotations in order polynomial times in the size $N$ of search space. We analyze the complexity of implementing the non-unitary operation and we observed that the price paid by performing this rotation is due the normalization. In Kraus operator approach we need $O(N)$ repetition of the algorithm to have a chance of measuring a solution in a post-selection, this is no better than the classical solution. However, the quantum singular value transform in addition with block encoding and Chebyshev polynomial approximation, we got complexity $O(\sqrt{N})$ and reach the Grover's bound with an extra resource of one single qubit, compared with the standard Grover's algorithm.
summary: This paper proposes a way to extend Grover's algorithm into a non-unitary regime by changing the metric tensor of the Hilbert space. While a simple non-unitary approach via Kraus operators results in no speedup over classical search, the authors demonstrate that using advanced techniques like the Quantum Singular Value Transform (QSVT) can achieve the optimal Grover complexity. This approach effectively performs the search in a single large rotation rather than many small ones, requiring only one extra ancilla qubit.
main_problem: The paper investigates a non-unitary extension of Grover's search algorithm to achieve a single-step solution by modifying the hidden geometry of the Hilbert space.
main_result: The authors show that while a Kraus operator approach fails to provide a quantum advantage, using the Quantum Singular Value Transform (QSVT) with block encoding and Chebyshev polynomial approximation allows the algorithm to reach the Grover bound with only one additional qubit.
method: The researchers use a geometric approach involving a generalized 2-rank tensor for the diffusion operator and analyze implementation via Kraus operators and QSVT-based block encoding.
model_or_system: The model is a generalized quantum amplitude amplification framework acting on an unstructured search space of size N, utilizing a non-unitary diffusion operator D_g and a Grover oracle U_f.
key_observables: Success probability (P_succ) and the rotation angle (theta) in the Hilbert space.
important_parameters: Search space size N, number of solutions M, rotation angles theta and phi, and the normalization factor p.
main_assumptions: The construction of the generalized operator requires prior knowledge of the number of solutions M; the implementation relies on the ability to perform block encoding.
paper_structure: The paper defines the geometric nature of the diffusion operator, reviews the standard Grover algorithm, develops the mathematical tools for the non-unitary extension, presents the new algorithm, and concludes with a complexity analysis.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
